example.title: Invalid Request
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The request could not be processed because the payload, parameters, or request structure is invalid.
commonCauses: The JSON body is malformed or does not match the endpoint schema.
Query parameters or request headers use unsupported values.
The request combines fields that cannot be used together.
clientRemediation: Compare the request against the endpoint documentation and schema.
Check the response `detail` field for request-specific guidance.
Remove unsupported fields or combinations before retrying.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retrying the same request will not help. Retry only after changing the request.

example.title: Missing Required Field
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The request is missing a field that the endpoint requires.
commonCauses: A required top-level property is absent from the request body.
A nested object is present but one of its required properties is missing.
A generated client or integration is sending an incomplete object.
clientRemediation: Add the required field identified by the response `detail` or field-level `errors`.
Regenerate or update client types if they are out of date with the API schema.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retrying without adding the missing field will continue to fail.

example.title: Invalid Parameter
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: A request parameter or field is present but has an invalid value.
commonCauses: A field has the wrong type, format, or enum value.
A string, list, or number is outside the allowed range.
A field-level validation error is included in the `errors` array.
clientRemediation: Use the response `detail` and any field-level `errors` to identify the invalid value.
Validate request values before sending them to the API.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry only after correcting the invalid value.

example.title: Invalid Pagination Cursor
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The pagination cursor cannot be used for this request.
commonCauses: The cursor is malformed, truncated, or copied incorrectly.
The cursor came from a different endpoint, query, or request shape.
The client changed pagination-related inputs between requests.
clientRemediation: Use cursors only with the same endpoint and request shape that returned them.
Start a new request and use the fresh cursor returned in that response.
remediation.retryGuidance: Do not retry with the same cursor. Start pagination again.

example.title: Expired Pagination Cursor
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The pagination cursor was valid previously but is no longer usable.
commonCauses: The cursor was stored and reused after its validity window ended.
The underlying result set changed enough that the cursor can no longer continue.
clientRemediation: Start a new search or list request and continue from the new cursor.
Avoid persisting cursors as long-lived bookmarks.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retrying with the expired cursor will fail. Retry from a fresh first page.

example.title: Invalid Filter
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: A filter in the request is not valid for the endpoint or datasource scope.
commonCauses: The filter field name is misspelled.
The field is not supported for the requested datasource or endpoint.
The filter value does not match the expected type or format.
clientRemediation: Check the endpoint documentation for supported filters.
Validate field names and values before sending the request.
Use response `detail` or field-level `errors` to identify the offending filter.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry only after correcting or removing the invalid filter.

example.title: Invalid Datasource
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The requested datasource scope is unknown, inaccessible, or not valid for the operation.
commonCauses: The datasource type or instance name is misspelled.
The caller does not have access to the datasource.
The request combines datasource scopes that the endpoint cannot process together.
clientRemediation: Verify datasource identifiers against the integration configuration.
Remove inaccessible or unsupported datasource scopes.
adminRemediation: Confirm the datasource is connected and visible to the integration.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry only after correcting the datasource scope or access.

example.title: Authentication Required
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The API request did not include valid authentication credentials.
commonCauses: The `Authorization` header is missing.
The token is malformed or not recognized.
The request is being sent to the wrong backend domain for the token.
clientRemediation: Include a valid bearer token in the `Authorization` header.
Confirm the token belongs to the Glean instance receiving the request.
adminRemediation: Create or rotate API tokens from the appropriate admin console workflow.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry after supplying valid credentials.

example.title: Token Expired
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The authentication token was valid but has expired.
commonCauses: An OAuth access token exceeded its lifetime.
A cached token was reused instead of refreshed.
clientRemediation: Refresh the token through the configured authentication flow.
Update token caching so expired tokens are not reused.
adminRemediation: Confirm the integration has an active credential configuration.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry after obtaining a fresh token.

example.title: Insufficient Permissions
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The caller is authenticated but is not allowed to perform the requested operation.
commonCauses: The token lacks the required scopes or permissions.
The authenticated user or app cannot access the requested resource.
The resource is intentionally hidden from callers without access.
clientRemediation: Check that the integration is using the intended token and scopes.
Use the response `detail` and endpoint documentation to identify required access.
adminRemediation: Grant the needed permissions or use a token associated with an authorized principal.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry only after permissions or scopes have been corrected.

example.title: Resource Not Found
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The requested resource or endpoint could not be found or is not visible to the caller.
commonCauses: The resource identifier is incorrect.
The resource exists but the caller does not have access.
The endpoint path or HTTP method does not match a supported API operation.
clientRemediation: Verify the endpoint path, resource ID, and HTTP method.
Treat hidden or unauthorized resources as indistinguishable from missing resources unless the endpoint documentation says otherwise.
adminRemediation: Confirm the resource exists and that the caller should have access to it.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry only after correcting the resource identifier, endpoint path, or permissions.

example.title: Method Not Allowed
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The endpoint exists but does not support the HTTP method used by the request.
commonCauses: The request uses `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, or `DELETE` incorrectly for the endpoint.
A client or proxy rewrote the method before forwarding the request.
clientRemediation: Use the HTTP method documented for the endpoint.
Confirm any proxy or API client preserves the intended method.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry with the supported method.

example.title: Request Timeout
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The server timed out while processing the request.
commonCauses: The request is too broad or expensive to complete within the timeout window.
A transient service or network delay occurred.
clientRemediation: Narrow the request when possible.
Use pagination or smaller batches for large operations.
remediation.retryGuidance: A retry may succeed if the timeout was transient. Use bounded retries with backoff.

example.title: Request Too Large
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The request body is larger than the API accepts.
commonCauses: A batch contains too many items.
A field contains unexpectedly large text or metadata.
The client is sending unnecessary data.
clientRemediation: Reduce the payload size.
Split large batches into smaller requests.
Remove fields the endpoint does not require.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry only after reducing the request size.

example.title: Conflict
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The request conflicts with the current state of the resource.
commonCauses: The resource already exists.
The requested update is stale or conflicts with another change.
The operation cannot be completed while the resource is in its current state.
clientRemediation: Fetch the latest resource state before retrying the change.
Adjust the request so it no longer conflicts with existing data.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retrying unchanged may continue to fail. Retry after reconciling state.

example.title: Gone
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The resource or operation is no longer available.
commonCauses: The resource was deleted.
The operation targets an expired or retired object.
The endpoint no longer supports the requested workflow.
clientRemediation: Stop using the stale resource or workflow.
Create a new resource or switch to the replacement documented by the endpoint.
remediation.retryGuidance: Do not retry the same request unless the resource is recreated or the workflow changes.

example.title: Unprocessable Query
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The API understood the request shape but could not process the query semantics.
commonCauses: The query is unsupported, ambiguous, or cannot be translated for the endpoint.
Query-related options conflict with each other.
clientRemediation: Simplify or rewrite the query.
Check endpoint documentation for supported query features.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry only after changing the query.

example.title: Rate Limit Exceeded
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The caller has sent too many requests in a time window.
commonCauses: The integration is sending bursts without throttling.
Multiple workers are sharing the same token or tenant limit.
Automatic retries are amplifying traffic.
clientRemediation: Back off before retrying.
Add client-side throttling and jitter.
Avoid retry loops that ignore rate-limit responses.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry after waiting. Use exponential backoff with jitter.

example.title: Internal Error
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The API encountered an unexpected error while processing the request.
commonCauses: A transient server-side problem occurred.
The request triggered a condition the API could not complete.
clientRemediation: Retry once or twice with backoff if the operation is safe to retry.
Record the `request_id` for support if the error persists.
adminRemediation: Share the `request_id`, timestamp, endpoint, and high-level request context with support.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry cautiously with backoff. Do not retry indefinitely.

example.title: Service Unavailable
example.detail: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

remediation.meaning: The API is temporarily unable to serve the request.
commonCauses: A transient availability issue is affecting the request.
The service is overloaded or temporarily unavailable.
clientRemediation: Retry with backoff.
Keep retries bounded so clients do not amplify service load.
remediation.retryGuidance: Retry with exponential backoff and jitter. Escalate if the issue persists.
